agent_scopes.mechanism: built-in roles always available; user-defined roles in config.toml [agents] section; user-defined roles override built-in roles of same name

definition_format.mechanism: TOML role config files referenced from config.toml [agents] section; built-in roles (default/explorer/worker) always available without user config

invocation_patterns.mechanism: spawn-agent tool at LLM discretion; LLM selects role from dynamically-built role descriptions combining built-in and user-defined roles

model_selection.mechanism: role config_file can override model and model_reasoning_effort; caller's current profile and model_provider preserved unless explicitly overridden

per_agent_mcp.mechanism: MCP configuration is workspace-wide; no per-role MCP scoping mechanism

subagent_spawning.mechanism: multi-agent V2 feature flag; max_threads controls concurrent agent count, max_depth controls nesting depth; spawn-agent tool initiates delegation

tool_restrictions.mechanism: role config layers override model/settings but no explicit tool allowlist or denylist in role definitions

providerExtensions.name: Agent Roles (config.toml [agents] section)
providerExtensions.description: Codex supports a multi-agent system where spawned sub-agents run with a named role. Roles are declared in config.toml under the agents key (AgentsToml). Each role entry (AgentRoleToml) specifies: description (human-facing role documentation used in spawn tool guidance), config_file (optional path to a role-specific config layer TOML), and nickname_candidates (candidate names for agents spawned with this role). The agents section also supports global limits: max_threads (concurrent agent threads cap), max_depth (maximum nesting depth for spawned threads, minimum 1), and job_max_runtime_seconds (default max runtime for agent job workers).

providerExtensions.name: Built-in Agent Roles (default, explorer, worker)
providerExtensions.description: Codex ships three built-in agent roles that are always available without user configuration. default: the standard agent with no special config. explorer: a fast, read-focused agent for codebase questions, runs with model_reasoning_effort=low and uses explorer.toml config; description encourages parallel spawning for independent questions. worker: an execution agent for implementing features, fixing bugs, and splitting large refactors; description emphasizes ownership assignment and awareness of parallel workers. A fourth role, awaiter (for monitoring long-running commands), exists as a built-in config (awaiter.toml) but is currently commented out of the active built-in registry.

providerExtensions.name: Role Config Layer
providerExtensions.description: When a role specifies a config_file, that file is loaded as a high-precedence config layer on top of the active session config. This allows roles to override model, reasoning effort, developer instructions, and other settings without modifying the user's base config. The role layer is inserted at session-flag precedence. The caller's current profile and model_provider are preserved unless the role explicitly selects them. Built-in role configs are embedded as static strings in the binary; user-defined role configs are read from disk paths relative to the config.toml that defines them.

providerExtensions.name: Multi-Agent V2 Feature Flag
providerExtensions.description: The multi-agent system is controlled by a feature flag: features.multi_agent_v2 in config.toml (FeatureToml_for_MultiAgentV2ConfigToml — either a boolean or a config object). The config object form supports: enabled (bool), hide_spawn_agent_metadata (bool), usage_hint_enabled (bool), and usage_hint_text (string). This allows operators to enable/disable multi-agent spawning and control how spawn metadata is surfaced.

providerExtensions.name: Spawn Tool Role Description Generation
providerExtensions.description: At session start, Codex dynamically builds the spawn-agent tool description by combining built-in and user-defined role descriptions and their locked settings (model and reasoning_effort if set in the role config). User-defined roles take precedence over built-in roles with the same name. This description is shown to the LLM to guide when and how to spawn sub-agents.
